context: Tests whether the agent adds a utility script for deterministic validation logic rather than relying on generated code each run. Validation of environment variables is deterministic and would otherwise be regenerated identically on each invocation — exactly the case that warrants a bundled script.

checklist.name: scripts/ directory created
checklist.description: The skill directory contains a scripts/ subdirectory

checklist.name: Validation script exists
checklist.description: At least one script file exists inside scripts/ that performs the env validation logic

checklist.name: Script is deterministic
checklist.description: The script implements fixed validation rules (presence checks, format checks, or placeholder detection) rather than being a template or stub that generates different code each time

checklist.name: SKILL.md references scripts
checklist.description: SKILL.md explicitly mentions or links to the script(s) in scripts/

checklist.name: Description has Use when
checklist.description: The frontmatter description includes 'Use when' with specific trigger conditions for the skill

checklist.name: Third-person description
checklist.description: The frontmatter description is written in third person

checklist.name: Quick start section
checklist.description: SKILL.md contains a Quick start section showing minimal usage

checklist.name: Concrete example
checklist.description: SKILL.md or the script includes at least one example showing sample input and expected output or error

checklist.name: Script not a stub
checklist.description: The script in scripts/ contains real implementation logic — NOT just TODO comments, placeholder functions, or empty shells that require the agent to generate code on each run
